name: azure
description: Inspect Azure subscriptions, resource groups, storage accounts, and function apps, and start or stop virtual machines via Azure Resource Manager

requiredApis.name: Azure Service Management API (Azure app registration)

envVars.name: MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: Microsoft Azure App Client ID (Application ID)

tools.name: List Subscriptions
tools.description: List all Azure subscriptions the signed-in user can access; use this first to get subscription IDs for the other tools

api-version.description: Azure Resource Manager API version for the subscriptions operation

tools.name: List Resource Groups
tools.description: List the resource groups in an Azure subscription

subscriptionId.description: Azure subscription ID (GUID) from List Subscriptions

api-version.description: Azure Resource Manager API version for the resource groups operation

$top.description: Maximum resource groups to return; omit to return all

$filter.description: Filter by tag, e.g. tagName eq 'env' and tagValue eq 'prod'

tools.name: List Virtual Machines
tools.description: List all virtual machines in an Azure subscription, optionally with runtime power state

subscriptionId.description: Azure subscription ID (GUID) from List Subscriptions

api-version.description: Azure Compute API version for the virtual machines operation

statusOnly.description: Set to 'true' to fetch the runtime status (power state) of all virtual machines in the subscription

tools.name: Start Virtual Machine
tools.description: Start a stopped or deallocated virtual machine (returns 202 Accepted; the operation completes asynchronously)

resourceGroupName.description: Resource group that contains the virtual machine

api-version.description: Azure Compute API version for the start operation

tools.name: Deallocate Virtual Machine
tools.description: Shut down a virtual machine and release its compute resources so it stops incurring compute charges (returns 202 Accepted)

resourceGroupName.description: Resource group that contains the virtual machine

api-version.description: Azure Compute API version for the deallocate operation

hibernate.description: Hibernate the virtual machine instead of fully deallocating it (VM must support hibernation)

tools.name: List Storage Accounts
tools.description: List all storage accounts in an Azure subscription (storage keys are not returned)

subscriptionId.description: Azure subscription ID (GUID) from List Subscriptions

api-version.description: Azure Storage resource provider API version for the storage accounts operation

tools.name: List Function and Web Apps
tools.description: List all App Service sites (function apps and web apps, Microsoft.Web/sites) in an Azure subscription; function apps have kind 'functionapp'

subscriptionId.description: Azure subscription ID (GUID) from List Subscriptions

api-version.description: Azure App Service API version for the sites operation

tools.name: Restart Virtual Machine
tools.description: Restart a running virtual machine (returns 202 Accepted; the operation completes asynchronously)

resourceGroupName.description: Resource group that contains the virtual machine

api-version.description: Azure Compute API version for the restart operation

tools.name: Get VM Instance View
tools.description: Get the runtime state of a single virtual machine, including power state (statuses with code PowerState/running etc.), OS info, and boot diagnostics — cheaper than listing all VMs with statusOnly

resourceGroupName.description: Resource group that contains the virtual machine

api-version.description: Azure Compute API version for the instance view operation

tools.name: List Resources
tools.description: List all resources in an Azure subscription across every resource type — a generic inventory beyond the dedicated list tools

subscriptionId.description: Azure subscription ID (GUID) from List Subscriptions

api-version.description: Azure Resource Manager API version for the resources operation

$filter.description: Filter expression, e.g. resourceType eq 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks', substringof('demo', name), or tagName eq 'env' and tagValue eq 'prod'

$expand.description: Comma-separated extra properties to include: createdTime, changedTime, provisioningState

$top.description: Maximum resources to return; omit to return all

prompts.title: Subscription inventory
prompts.prompt: List my Azure subscriptions and summarize the resource groups, virtual machines, storage accounts, and function apps in each one.

prompts.title: Audit VM power states
prompts.prompt: List my Azure virtual machines with their power states and point out any running VMs that look idle or forgotten.

prompts.title: Stop a VM to save cost
prompts.prompt: Help me deallocate an Azure virtual machine so it stops incurring compute charges, and confirm the operation was accepted.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Microsoft Azure setup

steps.title: Get an Azure subscription
steps.description: Sign in at https://portal.azure.com with a Microsoft account. New accounts can create a free subscription with trial credit at https://azure.microsoft.com/free, which is enough to test every tool in this connector.

steps.title: Register an Azure app
steps.description: In the Azure portal, open Microsoft Entra ID > App registrations > New registration. Choose 'Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts' (or your single tenant) and add your callback URL as a Web redirect URI.

steps.title: Add the Azure Service Management delegated permission
steps.description: Under API permissions, select Add a permission > Azure Service Management > Delegated permissions, and add user_impersonation. This lets the connector call Azure Resource Manager as the signed-in user.

steps.title: Create a client secret
steps.description: Under Certificates & secrets, create a client secret. Set MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID to the Application (client) ID and MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET to the secret value.

steps.title: Create test resources
steps.description: Create a resource group with a small VM (e.g. B1s) and a storage account so the list and VM power tools have something to return. Deallocate the VM when done to avoid charges.

notes: The signed-in user needs Azure RBAC roles on the subscription: Reader is enough for the list tools, and Virtual Machine Contributor (or Contributor) is required to start and deallocate VMs.
Start and deallocate are asynchronous: a 202 Accepted response means the operation was queued, not that it has finished; re-run List Virtual Machines with statusOnly=true to check the power state.
Deallocate (not just stop) releases the compute hardware so the VM stops incurring compute charges; disks continue to bill.
Each Azure resource provider versions its API independently, which is why every tool carries its own api-version default.
List Function and Web Apps returns all Microsoft.Web/sites resources; filter client-side on the 'kind' field (e.g. 'functionapp') to isolate function apps.
